title: AMD K5 CPU: Not quite what AMD hoped
content:
The K5 is AMD’s first x86 processor to be developed entirely in-house. Introduced March 27, 1996, its primary competition was Intel’s Pentium microprocessor. The K5 was an ambitious design, closer to a Pentium Pro internally than a Pentium. However, the

The post AMD K5 CPU: Not quite what AMD hoped appeared first on The Silicon Underground.
